mentions.note: Cheap at 17x earnings versus the S&P average and a huge beneficiary of 2026's M&A and IPO issuance boom; rallied nearly 4% with more room to run.
Extending 2025's rally as loosening regulation and rising M&A/equity issuance expand bank valuations.
Takeover/advisory business should be tremendous; expects a number increase.
Phenomenal blowout quarter; trades ridiculously low at 17x earnings despite world-class fundamentals; massive upside from current levels justified by standards and growth.
Cramer's favorite; pure play on investment banking at time when M&A and capital markets activity on fire.
Down ~10% Friday pre-Iran strike; Cramer and Jeff Marks both flagged 15x earnings as absurdly cheap; Lloyd Blankfein's 'Streetwise' explains valuation; way too cheap.
Better way to play gold/silver volatility and crisis markets than CME Group; Cramer's former employer excels in turmoil like current environment.

Multiple reasons to own: pro-deal Trump admin accelerates M&A advisory business for investment banks. Significant deal dry powder set to deploy. Reports next week expected strong; CNBC Investing Club has owned long-term.

Cramer's alma mater with big position in travel trust; well-positioned to profit from volatile markets through risk management; expects solid Q1 numbers and good reaction barring Iranian action weekend.
Blowout quarter reported, stock down 17% — take advantage of dip in quality bank.
Investment banking boom (M&A, IPOs) driving +14.9% earnings growth at 15× earnings; deep discount to S&P 500 valuation; admin M&A wave could unlock upside.
Big winner in IPO and M&A wave ahead; well-positioned for advisory fees and capital markets activity.
Goldman and Morgan Stanley winning big in hyperscaler stock derby with blockbuster deal flow; well-positioned for mega-cap technology capital raises.
Investment banking 'crushing it' on M&A advisory, IPO issuance, and bond underwriting; massive profit opportunity from $1.2T M&A and bond issuance pipeline.
Positioned for M&A advisory wave; huge advisory business; expect spectacular returns from merger activity under Trump anti-antitrust regime.
At 17x 2030 earnings (est. $20 EPS), valuation similar to prior $100 recommendation despite $1,000 share price. Upside to 1,700 possible without becoming expensive; hold for travel trust.
Cramer's favorite bank reports tomorrow but would need to vastly exceed consensus just to add ~4%; banks lack tech's upside.
Net income +78%, EPS +92%, investment banking +55%, FICC +32%, equities trading +72%; trades 18x earnings; top five position in charitable trust; early innings of deal cycle.
Cramer thought Goldman fabulous; financials show strength though one-day pop nature limits staying power.
High-quality financial name to own when tech is volatile; comprehensible business, not subject to Chinese rumors or margin compression.
